reasoning: The TLS 1.3 state machine enforces that KeyUpdate (SSL3_MT_KEY_UPDATE) is only a valid transition from state TLS_ST_OK, which is reached only after the handshake (including Finished) is complete. In ossl_statem_client13_read_transition (ssl/statem/statem_clnt.c, lines 180-195), SSL3_MT_KEY_UPDATE is only accepted in the 'case TLS_ST_OK:' branch. Similarly, in ossl_statem_server13_read_transition (ssl/statem/statem_srvr.c, lines 148-170), SSL3_MT_KEY_UPDATE is only accepted in 'case TLS_ST_OK:'. If a KeyUpdate arrives in any other state (i.e., before Finished), the function returns 0 (no valid transition). The callers — ossl_statem_client_read_transition (statem_clnt.c line 406) and ossl_statem_server_read_transition (statem_srvr.c line 312) — both have 'err:' labels that call SSLfatal(s, SSL3_AD_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE, SSL_R_UNEXPECTED_MESSAGE) and return 0. This causes the connection to be terminated with an unexpected_message alert, exactly as required by RFC 8446 section 4.6.3.

evidence.expected_behavior: If KeyUpdate is received before the Finished message (i.e., before state TLS_ST_OK), the implementation must terminate with an unexpected_message alert.
